title: IoT Smart Home Central Hub
tips: Isolate high-voltage relay contacts in a physical enclosure to prevent electric shock.
Subscribe to home/control topics to receive override signals from mobile apps instantly.

title: IoT LDR Smart Street Lights
connections: NodeMCU D1 (PWM) → TIP120 Base via 1kΩ resistor
tips: Increase PWM frequency to prevent visible LED flickering.
Protect LDR sensors from receiving light directly from the controlled LED to prevent oscillation.

title: IoT Smart City Noise/Light Node
connections: Microphone OUT → NodeMCU A0 (via analog multiplexer or single read)
tips: The ESP8266 analog pin only tolerates voltages up to 1.0V. Verify your module includes an onboard voltage divider.
Sample noise levels in short bursts to capture peak audio transients.

title: IoT Smart Air Quality Monitor
connections: Dust Sensor Led → GPIO 12, Vo → GPIO 35
tips: MQ gas sensors require a burn-in period of 24-48 hours before returning accurate calibrated measurements.
The dust sensor internal LED needs a specific pulse profile (0.28ms pulse width) to record particles.

title: IoT RS485 Modbus Sensor Node
connections: MAX485 A/B → Modbus Sensor A/B lines
tips: RS-485 is a half-duplex bus; toggle the DE/RE direction pin HIGH to transmit and LOW to receive Modbus frames.
Use twisted-pair shielded cabling to maintain communications over industrial distances.

title: IoT Water Quality Sensor Node
connections: pH Sensor Analog Out → ESP32 GPIO32
DS18B20 Data line → ESP32 GPIO15 (+ 4.7kΩ pull-up)
tips: ph probes require periodic calibration in buffer solutions (pH 4.0, 7.0, and 10.0) to maintain measurement accuracy.
Temperature affects pH readings. Integrate DS18B20 temp values to calculate temperature-compensated pH values.

title: IoT Patient Health Alert Node
tips: The MAX30102 is highly sensitive to motion artifacts. Keep fingers still on the glass transducer when taking readings.
The heart rate algorithm requires fast sampling rates (at least 100Hz) to correctly map plethysmographic curves.
